user_prompt: are you able to see the console log with the errors? and the changes to platformio.ini?
agent_reply: - Added `custom_sdkconfig` for HCI SCO / HFP audio:
- `theflyinfolog` is now `CORE_DEBUG_LEVEL=3`, so `ESP_LOGI` should be enabled.
2. The current build errors from rerunning `pio run -e theflybringuptest`.
The important part: your `custom_sdkconfig` is working enough that PlatformIO now says:
*** Compile Arduino IDF libs for theflybringuptest ***
So it started rebuilding the Arduino/IDF Bluetooth sources. That’s good news.
The new problem is include-path poisoning. Our global build flags include:
So when ESP-IDF’s own Bluedroid source files compile, they accidentally include **our** copied SBC headers instead of the ESP-IDF internal ones. That causes the signature mismatch errors like:
lib/bluedroid/encoder/include/sbc_encoder.h:31:14: fatal error: bt_target.h: No such file or directory

So the next fix is probably to stop exposing `lib/bluedroid/decoder/include` and `lib/bluedroid/encoder/include` globally from `platformio.ini`, and make those private to the local `bluedroid-msbc` library instead. That way your app can build the local mSBC wrapper, but ESP-IDF’s Bluetooth stack won’t accidentally ingest our headers.

I'm guessing this is an improvement
first, regardless of if you think mSBC is still needed, I want to fix this crash so the code isn't containing any ticking time bombs
agent_reply: The big one: `hfp_outgoing_audio()` now only calls the userspace mSBC encoder after the callback payload has been positively detected as encoded mSBC, not while it is still `Unknown`: [AudioManager.cpp](</d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/AudioManager/AudioManager.cpp:1039>). That prevents the speculative encoder call that led into `EncPacking`.

I also fixed the deeper ticking time bomb: the bundled Bluedroid mSBC wrapper was getting linked against ESP-IDF’s `SBC_Encoder` symbol. I added a local symbol prefix header so the bundled wrapper calls its own bundled encoder ABI: [sbc_symbol_prefix.h](</d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/bluedroid/encoder/include/sbc_symbol_prefix.h:1>) and included it from [sbc_encoder.h](</d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/bluedroid/encoder/include/sbc_encoder.h:28>). I checked the final map and `bluedroid_sbc_encode` now points to `bluedroid_local_SBC_Encoder`.

I also kept Bluetooth “ready” false until after `AudioManager::setHfpAudioFormat()` finishes, so the outgoing callback cannot race codec setup: [BluetoothManager.cpp](</d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/BluetoothManager/BluetoothManager.cpp:619>).

user_prompt: if our copy inside `lib\bluedroid\encoder` and `lib\bluedroid\decoder`, then block our own copy and use the included one from esp-idf
agent_reply: Done. I blocked our local `lib/bluedroid/encoder` and `lib/bluedroid/decoder` sources from compiling and switched the wrapper to use ESP-IDF’s included Bluedroid SBC symbols instead.
- `platformio.ini` now exposes the ESP-IDF BT/SBC include paths
- Removed the earlier local symbol-prefix workaround

I also fixed the wrapper for the ESP-IDF encoder shape, including copying PCM into `as16PcmBuffer` when `SBC_NO_PCM_CPY_OPTION` is false. That is likely the actual crash fix for the `EncPacking` StoreProhibited path.
- The map file now shows SBC encoder/decoder symbols coming from ESP-IDF `libbt.a`, while our library only contributes `bluedroid_sbc.c` as the adapter.

I also fixed the wrapper for the ESP-IDF encoder shape, including copying PCM into `as16PcmBuffer` when `SBC_NO_PCM_CPY_OPTION` is false. That is likely the actual crash fix for the `EncPacking` StoreProhibited path.
- The map file now shows SBC encoder/decoder symbols coming from ESP-IDF `libbt.a`, while our library only contributes `bluedroid_sbc.c` as the adapter.
